text: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day, listening to the murmur of the water, is by no means a waste of time.
author: John Lubbock

text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
author: Anne Lamott

text: Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit. Then get back to work.
author: Ralph Marston

text: The time to relax is when you don't have time for it.
author: Sydney J. Harris

text: Take rest; a field that has rested gives a bountiful crop.
author: Ovid

text: You can't pour from an empty cup. Take care of yourself first.
Rest is not quitting the busy career; rest is the fitting of self to its sphere.
author: John Sullivan Dwight

text: Even the sun sets every evening to rise again the next morning.
Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against your challenges.
author: Bryant McGill

text: There is virtue in work and there is virtue in rest. Use both and overlook neither.
author: Alan Cohen

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Beware the barrenness of a busy life.
author: Socrates

text: In our constant rush, we forget that we are human beings, not human doings.
The trees that are slow to grow bear the best fruit.
author: Molière

text: Slow down and everything you are chasing will come around and catch you.
author: John De Paola

text: There is more to life than increasing its speed.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Doing nothing is better than being busy doing nothing.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Never be so busy as not to think of others.
author: Mother Teresa

text: It's not enough to be busy. The question is: what are we busy about?
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: If you get tired, learn to rest, not to quit.
author: Banksy

text: Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is relax.
author: Mark Black

text: He who would travel happily must travel light.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: It is not the load that breaks you down, it is the way you carry it.
author: Lou Holtz

text: Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
author: William James

text: When you rest, you catch your breath and it holds you up, like water wings.
author: Anne Lamott

text: The bow kept forever taut will break.
author: Phaedrus

text: What is this life if, full of care, we have no time to stand and stare?
author: W.H. Davies

text: For fast-acting relief, try slowing down.
author: Lily Tomlin

text: Half of the troubles of this life can be traced to saying yes too quickly and not saying no soon enough.
author: Josh Billings

text: You don't always need a plan. Sometimes you just need to breathe, trust, let go, and see what happens.
author: Mandy Hale

text: Surrender to what is. Let go of what was. Have faith in what will be.
author: Sonia Ricotti

text: The greatest mistake you can make in life is to be continually fearing you will make one.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: Not all storms come to disrupt your life. Some come to clear your path.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: Be gentle with yourself. You're doing the best you can.
Talk to yourself like you would to someone you love.
author: Brené Brown

text: Self-care is not selfish. You cannot serve from an empty vessel.
author: Eleanor Brownn

text: Caring for myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation.
author: Audre Lorde

text: You are allowed to be both a masterpiece and a work in progress simultaneously.
author: Sophia Bush

text: The most powerful relationship you will ever have is the relationship with yourself.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: Burnout is what happens when you try to avoid being human for too long.
author: Michael Gungor

text: You are not a machine. You are a garden. You need tending.
Exhaustion is not a status symbol.
author: Brené Brown

text: The candle that burns twice as bright burns half as long.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Being busy is a form of laziness — lazy thinking and indiscriminate action.
author: Tim Ferriss

text: Burnout is nature's way of telling you you've been going through the motions.
author: Sam Keen

text: Wherever you are, be there totally.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Breathing in, I calm body and mind. Breathing out, I smile.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
author: Ram Dass

text: Within you, there is a stillness and a sanctuary to which you can retreat at any time.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: In today's rush, we all think too much, seek too much, want too much, and forget about the joy of just being.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: You are the sky. Everything else is just the weather.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: When you realize nothing is lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
author: Lao Tzu

text: All of humanity's problems stem from man's inability to sit quietly in a room alone.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: Learn to be calm and you will always be happy.
author: Paramahansa Yogananda

text: The soul always knows what to do to heal itself. The challenge is to silence the mind.
author: Caroline Myss

text: To a mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Silence is a source of great strength.
author: Lao Tzu

text: In the middle of movement and chaos, keep stillness inside of you.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: Be still. Stillness reveals the secrets of eternity.
author: Lao Tzu

text: We are human beings, not human doings.
At the end of the day, remind yourself that you did the best you could today, and that is enough.
The world is full of magical things patiently waiting for our wits to grow sharper.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: A day without laughter is a day wasted.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: Don't be pushed around by the fears in your mind. Be led by the dreams in your heart.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Give yourself permission to slow down. Not everything is urgent.
Sometimes the bravest thing you can do is rest.
Your peace is more important than driving yourself crazy trying to understand why something happened the way it did.
author: Mandy Hale

text: You have permission to rest. You are not responsible for fixing everything that is broken.
Just breathe. You are enough. You have enough. You do enough.
The world will not end if you take a break.
Do not rush. The right thing happens at the right time.
You owe yourself the love that you so freely give to other people.
No one is more deserving of your kindness than you.
Put yourself at the top of your to-do list every single day and the rest will fall into place.
It's okay to not be okay, as long as you are not giving up.
author: Karen Salmansohn

text: Stop. Breathe. Look around. This is where you need to be.
The greatest prayer is patience.
author: Buddha

text: Close your eyes. Clear your heart. Let it go.
Pause. Breathe. Repair your universe. Proceed.
Don't confuse being busy with being productive.
Work without rest is like a bow without a string — useless.
If you don't make time for your wellness, you will be forced to make time for your illness.
Your body is not an apology. Your rest is not a weakness.
Be where you are, not where you think you should be.
Stillness is not the absence of motion. It is the presence of peace.
Your greatest self has been waiting your whole life. Don't make it wait any longer.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: You were not born to just pay bills and die.
The only way to do great work is to love what you do. And sometimes, love means stepping away.
Life is what happens between coffee and wine.
We do not stop because we are tired. We are tired because we do not stop.
It is okay to do nothing and just breathe.
You don't have to earn rest. Rest is your right.
Imagine your thoughts as clouds drifting across a vast sky. You don't need to chase them. Just watch them pass.
Notice your breath — don't change it, just notice it. In and out, like waves on a shore.
In the quiet, solutions find you. Problems that seemed impossible often dissolve when the mind is still.
Rest is not the opposite of progress. It is the foundation of it.
Your nervous system has been in overdrive. This is permission to switch off.
You don't need to earn this break. You don't need to justify it. You are enough, exactly as you are, right now.
